title: senna gundlachii subsp. esmeraldensis
intro: Senna gundlachii subsp. esmeraldensis, also known as Mexican senna or Esmeralda senna, is a plant species belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae) and is native to Mexico and Central America. The medicinal properties of Senna gundlachii subsp. esmeraldensis include its laxative and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat constipation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The herbal preparations of Senna gundlachii subsp. esmeraldensis include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
